title: quercus rubra var. palustris
intro: Quercus rubra var. palustris, also known as the swamp white oak, is a variety of the white oak tree native to North America, characterized by its deciduous nature and ability to thrive in wet environments. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Quercus rubra var. palustris include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and protect against certain types of cancer, due to its high levels of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. Herbal preparations of Quercus rubra var. palustris include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
